title: Quiz: Quantum Finance Applications
description: Test your understanding of quantum computing in finance

questions.question: Which finance application is most promising for near-term quantum computing?

options.text: Portfolio optimization on small asset sets

questions.explanation: Portfolio optimization on small numbers of assets (5-20) maps naturally to quantum optimization and is feasible on current hardware.

questions.question: What speedup does quantum amplitude estimation offer for Monte Carlo?

options.text: Quadratic speedup (√N instead of N samples)

questions.explanation: Quantum amplitude estimation provides quadratic speedup, requiring √N quantum queries instead of N classical samples.

questions.question: What is VaR (Value at Risk)?

options.text: Maximum loss at a given confidence level

questions.explanation: VaR is the maximum expected loss at a specified confidence level (e.g., 95% confidence that losses won't exceed VaR).

questions.question: Quantum computers currently outperform classical methods for practical finance applications.
questions.explanation: False. Classical methods are highly optimized and currently outperform quantum approaches for all practical finance applications.

questions.question: How is portfolio optimization mapped to quantum computing?

options.text: Formulating as QUBO and using QAOA/quantum annealing

questions.explanation: Portfolio optimization is formulated as a Quadratic Unconstrained Binary Optimization (QUBO) problem, then solved with QAOA or quantum annealing.

questions.question: What limits quantum advantage for amplitude estimation on NISQ devices?

options.text: Finance problems don't need Monte Carlo

questions.explanation: NISQ noise corrupts the coherent operations needed for amplitude estimation, often negating the theoretical quadratic speedup.

questions.question: What is a realistic near-term strategy for financial institutions?

options.text: Immediately replace all systems with quantum
Build capabilities with small experiments while maintaining classical systems

questions.explanation: The best strategy is building quantum literacy and experimenting on small problems while keeping robust classical production systems.

questions.question: Which are challenges for quantum option pricing? (Select all)

options.text: Deep circuits needed to load probability distributions
Amplitude estimation requires many controlled operations

questions.explanation: Quantum option pricing faces deep circuit requirements, competition from fast classical methods, and complex amplitude estimation circuits.
